key_insight: ML-driven molecular dynamics shows that MgH2 dehydrogenation is governed by subsurface H2 formation and that Ni is the best dopant for accelerating hydrogen release.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a quantitative design rule for selecting catalyst/dopant chemistry to improve hydrogen-storage materials and hydrogen-pump performance.
method: ML potential-driven molecular dynamics on pristine and doped MgH2
Surface-orientation screening to identify the most active low-index facet
Descriptor learning across 22 dopants to link electronic structure with H release
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic simulations, 22-dopant screening, and a volcano trend with an optimal Miedema electron-density window for hydrogen release.
watch_item: The conclusions rely on ML-MD fidelity and descriptor transferability, so experimental validation and broader materials testing are still needed.
paper_title: Machine Learning Potential-Driven Molecular Dynamics Simulations of Dehydrogenation in Pristine and Doped MgH$_2$
